zh.Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可斯托克 白 納易藍德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Ehler Goodwyn於1497年出生在英國素福爾可斯托克 白 納易藍德時，她的父親Edward Braddrock Goodwyn27歲，母親Elizabeth Brown29歲。她於1511年與George Abbes在英國素福爾可納易藍德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1572年9月19日在英國素福爾可納易藍德享年75歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Ehler Goodwyn于1497年出生在英国素福爾可斯托克 白 納易藍德时，她的父亲Edward Braddrock Goodwyn27岁，母亲Elizabeth Brown29岁。她于1511年与George Abbes在英国素福爾可納易藍德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1572年9月19日在英国素福爾可納易藍德享年75岁。

nameMeanings.name: Goodwyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Anglia and Middlesex): variant of   Goodwin  .

variants.name: Goodwin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Godwin (Old English Gōdwine). Compare Godwin .
Irish: adopted in Mayo for Ó Goidín, a variant of Ó Godáin, ‘descendant of Godán’, a diminutive personal name from got ‘stammering, lisping’.

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. William Goodwin was one of the founders of Hartford, CT, in 1635 (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Ehler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Ehlert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Alois, Benno, Dieter, Ernst, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Joerg, Juergen.
North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements agil ‘point, edge (of a sword)’ + hard ‘brave, hardy, strong’ or ward ‘guard’. Compare Elert and Ellert .

variants.name: Ehlers
meanings: North German: patronymic from   Ehler   or   Ehlert  .

variants.name: Aylor
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin an occupational name for a tailor (Anglo-Norman taillour, a derivative of taillier ‘to cut’, Late Latin taleare). Use as a given name was influenced by the U.S. president Zachary Taylor ( 1784–1850 ), hero of the Mexican War. As a girl's name it became well established in North America in the 1980s and has since also taken root in Britain.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Ehler Goodwyn was born in 1497, in Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England, her father, Edward Braddrock Goodwyn, was 27 and her mother, Elizabeth Brown, was 29. She married George Abbes in 1511, in Nayland, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 19 September 1572, in Nayland, Suffolk, England, at the age of 75.

Elle a épousé George Abbes en 1511, à Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 19 septembre 1572, à Nayland, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans.

ko.Stoke, Nayland, Suffolk, England, United: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 스토크 비 이프스위치
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Ehler Goodwyn(이)가 1497년 영국 수폴크 스토크 비 나이랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward Braddrock Goodwyn(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Brown(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1511년 영국 수폴크 나이랜드에서 George Abbes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1572년 9월 19일 영국 수폴크 나이랜드에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Ehler Goodwyn родилась в 1497 г. в местности сток би неиланд, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Edward Braddrock Goodwyn было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Brown, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Abbes в 1511 г. в местности неиланд, суффолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 19 сентября 1572 г. в местности неиланд, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 75 лет.
